codeReview.summary: Reviewed the rerun against the prior failure. The current runtime boundary now blocks IPv4-mapped IPv6 loopback targets: `mini/lib/runtime-config.js:7-11,89-110` adds a shared `net.BlockList` with the mapped-loopback subnet `::ffff:7f00:0/104`, `mini/tests/runtime-config-boundary.test.mjs:87-105` covers `[::ffff:127.0.0.1]`, `[::ffff:127.255.255.255]`, and `[::ffff:7f00:1]`, and `mini/scripts/validate-mini.mjs:155-156` still enforces checked-in runtime targets through `isLoopbackUrl()`. An independent Node probe against HEAD returned `true` for those mapped loopback spellings while leaving `https://[::ffff:128.0.0.1]` and `https://mini-runtime.example.com` as non-loopback. The earlier runtime.local.js failure handling also remains intact: `mini/lib/runtime-config.js:122-155` still only treats the override module itself as optional, so broken dependency/import failures continue to surface, and `mini/tests/runtime-config-boundary.test.mjs:54-85` still asserts that behavior. I did not find any new high-confidence blocking or non-blocking issues in the current diff/state for this feature.

summary: Pass. The rerun fixes the prior blocker: IPv4-mapped IPv6 loopback targets such as `[::ffff:127.0.0.1]` are now rejected by the shared runtime boundary and by the publish-shell validator path, while broken `mini/config/runtime.local.js` dependency/import failures still surface instead of falling back. No new high-confidence issues were found.
